decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will slightly increase the heat spreader conductivity. This lever has high priority and remaining headroom (current 500 W/mK is at the upper bound of typical materials but the editable range allows up to 500, wait, current is 500. Let me re-read bounds. Bounds=[20.0, 500.0]. The current value is 500.0. I cannot increase conductivity further. I must switch to geometry. I will increase the spreader width to improve lateral spreading area.

changes.reason: Conductivity is already at its maximum bound (500). Increasing the spreader width to the maximum allowed (1.0m) will maximize the lateral area for heat dissipation, helping to lower the peak chip temperature further.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the lateral direction
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
risk_notes: Spreader width is approaching the design domain limit; ensure it does not overlap with boundary conditions improperly
Diminishing returns may occur as the spreader approaches the full domain width
Geometric change may require mesh adaptation in future steps if gradients become steep near edges
